title: Unexpected result when using range comparison with `NULL`
description: Consider the test case below. It is unexpected that the second query returns 1, since the `BETWEEN` expression is evaluated to `false`, and thus the result should be an empty table.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value INTEGER, range_start INTEGER, range_end INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, range_start, range_end) VALUES (1, 1, NULL, -1);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, range_start, range_end) VALUES (2, 2, 0, 2);
SELECT (CASE WHEN value BETWEEN range_start AND range_end THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS is_within_range FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE (value BETWEEN range_start AND range_end) IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Range comparison with NULL in BETWEEN expression
